title: [release-v4.3.1-rhel] compat,build: handle docker's preconfigured `cacheTo`,`cacheFrom`
body:
Docker's newer clients popuates `cacheFrom` and `cacheTo` parameter by default as empty array for all commands but buildah's design of distributed cache expects this to be a repo not image hence parse only the first populated repo and igore if empty array.

```release-note
compat,build: make compat build api functional
```

Backport of https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16380

Honestly, I'm not sure why we have this branch as 4.3 is not in RHEL at the moment, nor is it planned to be....  Let's discuss at the next standup.
@TomSweeneyRedHat can/should we remove the branch entirely? Did you talk to QE?

Hi,

Actually I was wrong thinking this issue started with podman 4.3, it's present with podman 4.2 which  has hit RHEL already. We start to have the issue as it seems we have upgraded our machines only recently (we should definitely upgrade more often...). I guess it's hopeless to have it backported in branch RHEL branch 4.2 and have an official delivery ?

If yes, I will just backport this to 4.2, rebuild the package myself, and distributed to all our RHEL machines instead of the official Red Hat package.

Cheers,
Romain
